summary: Closed, unmerged PR proposing an AI-assisted decompilation batch scoped to 8 claimed 100% byte-perfect function matches across Crazy Hand, Ice Mountain, Pura, and Old Kuri item code. The diff replaces stubs or partial bodies with matched-looking C, including pointer-style field access, PAD_STACK, a motion-state enum correction, stage animation/collision callbacks, and item state setup. Later closure comment says most changes were covered by other PRs.

key_files.role: Changed ftCh_Damage2_Anim motion-state enum from ftMh_MS_Damage to ftMh_MS_Damage2; PR body lists this as one of 8 100% matches.
Largest change set; implements or rewrites Ice Mountain material loops, animation state machine, and collision callback variants with pointer offsets, GroundVars access, and animation helper calls.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: grIceMt_801F7F1C, grIceMt_801F81B4, grIceMt_801F929C, fn_801F9338, fn_801F9448 hunks

key_files.role: Rewrites grPura_80212290 stage setup to set a callback, toon texture image, JObj setup, and stage animation call.
Fills it_802D747C stub with Old Kuri item setup/state-change call sequence.

what_changed.text: PR narrowed scope to 8 claimed byte-perfect function matches after earlier feedback about partial improvements and pointer math.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: decomp-dev[bot] report lists 8 new matches

what_changed.text: ftCh_Damage2_Anim now passes ftMh_MS_Damage2 rather than ftMh_MS_Damage to Fighter_ChangeMotionState.
grIceMt_801F7F1C and grIceMt_801F81B4 were rewritten as two-iteration loops over adjacent ground data, checking a pointer at offset 0xF8 and calling grMaterial_801C8CDC when non-null.

grIceMt_801F929C was expanded into a small animation-state routine that increments a s16 counter, conditionally calls mpLib_80057BC0 or mpJointListAdd, and clears a flag when grAnime_801C83D0 finishes.

fn_801F9338 and fn_801F9448 were expanded from commented placeholder calls into ground collision callbacks that inspect a CollData flag byte, initialize Ice Mountain state fields, start animations, then tail-call grIceMt_801FA7F0.
grPura_80212290 was changed from cleaner typed locals to temp-style locals plus PAD_STACK, direct callback-slot write at gobj offset 0x1C, grPu_803E7620 image use, and NULL toon texture reset.
it_802D747C changed from an empty stub to calls to it_8027CAD8, it_8027C0A8(gobj, 0.0f, 5.0f), it_802756E0, and it_802D848C(gobj, 0, 2).

smart_moves.text: Author explicitly narrowed the submission to claimed 100% byte-perfect function matches and removed non-100% functions after review feedback, reducing review risk for an AI-assisted pipeline.
Author documented verification claims separately: ninja with matching DOL SHA1, melee-issues with no new issues, EditorConfig cleanup, and objdiff checks for all 8 functions.
BR-'s review guidance gave concrete pipeline improvements instead of only rejecting generated-looking code: m2c --valid-syntax --no-casts, union-field hints, and local naming conventions.

decomp_lessons.text: For AI-assisted decompilation submissions, keep PR scope narrow and remove partial, pointer-math-heavy functions unless they are ready for review and backed by match evidence.
A 100% objdiff claim does not by itself make pointer-style access ideal source; reviewers still pushed toward M2C_FIELD or recovered struct fields and union arms.
Header inline recognition matters for generated assert-looking code: the author recorded feedback to recognize jobj.h inline/assert boundaries rather than reimplementing assertion logic.
When path/module context implies a vars union, provide m2c union-field hints so generated output starts closer to real GroundVars, FighterVars, or ItemVars access.

naming_conventions.text: Use short role names such as gp, jobj, fp, and i when the role is clear; keep placeholder/temp names when meaning is not obvious.
The diff still contains temp_r30/temp_r31/temp_r4 and raw ptr names in some matched functions, making this PR useful evidence of the review tension between matching and readable source naming.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: Used PAD_STACK(8) and dummy u8 _[8] locals in several claimed matches, indicating stack-shape steering; standards require narrow verifier evidence for such tactics.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: grIceMt_801F7F1C, grIceMt_801F81B4, grPura_80212290, it_802D747C hunks

assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: Used direct pointer arithmetic and casts for offsets such as 0xF8, 0x100, 0x34, and gobj+0x1C where types or fields were not fully recovered in the diff.
BR- corrected m2c flag guidance: --valid-syntax generates M2C_FIELD, while --no-casts reduces surrounding casts; both can be used together.
Author reported adding path-based automatic union-field detection in their pipeline: /gr/ to GroundVars, /ft/ to FighterVars, and /it/ to ItemVars.
Bot report recorded +1292 matched-code bytes and listed the same 8 new function matches, but the PR was later closed unmerged.

review_feedback.text: Author says earlier reviewer feedback from @ribbanya led to removing non-100% functions, removing automated Copilot review, and noting jobj.h inline plus M2C_FIELD guidance; direct @ribbanya comments are not present in the loaded slice.
BR- advised that --valid-syntax, not --no-casts alone, generates M2C_FIELD; using both --valid-syntax and --no-casts yields cleaner M2C_FIELD output.
BR- identified FighterVars and ItemVars as other major union-field targets besides GroundVars.

BR- noted ambiguity around Ice Mountain offsets near xF8: grIceMt_801F87FC treats it as HSD_GObj*, fn_801F9338 treats it as s16, and grIceMt_GroundVars2 stops at xF4, suggesting possible unmapped s16 data after that point.

Copilot automated review comments in the loaded slice flagged issues in earlier or removed code paths, including allocation overflow/free-lifetime risk, u32* pointer increment scaling, null dereference, stack out-of-bounds raw offset write, and OSReport varargs mismatch; author later said automated reviews were removed.
PsiLupan closed the PR because most changes had been covered by other PRs; loaded evidence does not identify which other PRs covered each function.

follow_up_queries: PR 2373 functions covered by other PRs PsiLupan closing comment
grIceMt_GroundVars2 xF4 xF8 x100 field recovery
m2c valid-syntax no-casts M2C_FIELD union-field GroundVars FighterVars ItemVars
jobj.h inline assert recognition HSD_ASSERT pipeline
it_802D747C Old Kuri state change sequence later PR

evidence_refs: raw_diff: complete diff excerpt for 4 changed files
human_pr_text: full PR body and issue comments
text_corpus: bot report and issue comments
notes: The PR was closed with merged_at null, so changed code should not be treated as accepted upstream source from this PR.
Verification claims are present in the PR body and decomp-dev bot report, but this intake did not run builds or objdiff.
Direct @ribbanya review text is not loaded; only the author's summary of that feedback is available.

Review_comments excerpt is truncated and includes Copilot comments for files not in the final changed_files list, likely earlier revision context; do not treat those comments as applying to the final 4-file diff without more history.
Closure comment says most changes were covered by other PRs, but the slice does not identify the replacement PRs.

accepted_candidate_records.title: AI-assisted decomp PRs need narrow, verified scope
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2373 is evidence that AI-assisted decomp submissions were pushed toward small batches of claimed 100% byte-perfect matches and away from partial pointer-math-heavy functions.

accepted_candidate_records.title: m2c flag guidance from review discussion
accepted_candidate_records.text: BR- clarified that m2c --valid-syntax generates M2C_FIELD and --no-casts cleans surrounding casts; both may be used together as a better starting point than raw pointer casts.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Path-based union hints discussed for generated decomp output
accepted_candidate_records.text: The PR discussion identifies GroundVars, FighterVars, and ItemVars as major union-field targets and records the author's plan to choose them by source path.

source_update_candidates.title: Consider adding m2c flag note for M2C_FIELD generation
source_update_candidates.text: Review discussion in PR #2373 says --valid-syntax is what generates M2C_FIELD, and --valid-syntax --no-casts produces cleaner M2C_FIELD output than raw pointer casts. This may belong in tooling guidance rather than source style standards.
source_update_candidates.reason: Could make existing typed-fields-over-pointer-math guidance more actionable for generated starting points, but needs curator decision because it is tool-specific.

source_update_candidates.title: Consider documenting union-field hint workflow
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2373 discussion suggests using --union-field GroundVars:icemt for ground stage files, with FighterVars and ItemVars as other major union-field families. Author later described automatic path-based detection: /gr/ -> GroundVars, /ft/ -> FighterVars, /it/ -> ItemVars.
source_update_candidates.reason: Useful pipeline guidance, but should not be promoted as a universal rule without checking current tooling docs and path exceptions.

source_update_candidates.title: Ice Mountain GroundVars2 xF8/x100 ambiguity
source_update_candidates.text: BR- observed that offsets near xF8 in gricemt are ambiguous: grIceMt_801F87FC treats one access as HSD_GObj*, fn_801F9338 treats nearby data as s16, and grIceMt_GroundVars2 stops at xF4, suggesting unmapped s16 data after it.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: grIceMt_801F7F1C, grIceMt_801F81B4, fn_801F9338, fn_801F9448 hunks
source_update_candidates.reason: Potentially valuable local type-recovery note, but it is a reviewer hypothesis and needs current-source verification before becoming a path fact.

source_update_candidates.title: AI-assisted PR intake should exclude automated review noise
source_update_candidates.text: Author reported removing automated Copilot review and not including it in future PRs after reviewer feedback. Loaded review comments show automated findings on code that appears outside the final changed_files list.
source_update_candidates.reason: Could be process guidance for contributors, but evidence of reviewer intent is indirect because the original reviewer comment is not loaded.

rejection_notes: Do not mark the 8 function bodies from this PR as accepted source facts; PR was closed unmerged and closure says most changes were covered elsewhere.
Do not treat author-reported ninja, matching DOL SHA1, melee-issues, or objdiff results as independently validated by this intake.
Do not generalize the raw pointer arithmetic or PAD_STACK shapes as approved style; standards require typed fields and explicit verifier evidence for matching tactics.
Do not promote Copilot review comments on lbcardnew.c, it_2725.c, ifstatus.c, ftmaterial.c, or ftcoll.c as final-review findings for the final 4-file diff without reconstructing the earlier revision.
